summarization.a: The chart shows the thickness of WO₃ as a function of the number of cycles at different temperatures (128°C, 160°C, 196°C, and 207°C). The thickness decreases linearly with increasing cycle number for each temperature.

a.question: What temperature results in the highest EPC and how can this be concluded?
a.answer: a	The highest temperature will also result in the highest EPC, this can be concluded based on the slopes of the different lines.

a.question: What is the EPC for the process at 160 degrees Celcius?
is there a linear trend in increase in EPC with increasing temperature?
What processing temperature will result in the highest control in etching?
a.answer: a	This will be determined by the height of the EPC. This is the lowest for the process at 128 degrees. This process will then also result in the most control.
